footer.line3: Address: 6th floor, 214, Yulgok-ro, Jongno-gu, Seoul, Korea

home.overviewBody: ROFORMAN develops practical robotic systems for medical and assistive environments. Our team combines hardware design, control engineering, and validation workflows to deliver reliable technologies from lab prototypes to real-world use.
home.cap1: Infusion pump prototype and dosage workflow test setup.
home.cap2: Wearable assistive robot evaluation in a lab environment.
home.cap3: Foldable mobility platform prototype and compact mechanism study.
home.cap4: Humanoid robot demonstration from platform research work.
home.cap5: Stair-climbing mobility test with stability verification.
home.cap6: Exoskeleton control test focused on precision and response.
home.aboutMain: ROFORMAN Inc. develops various robots that assist humans.
home.aboutDesc: As an in-house startup of Seoul National University Hospital (Medical Robotics and Actuator Systems Laboratory), ROFORMAN Inc. selects items with high demand and solvable by robotic technology from among the unmet needs of the medical field requested by all clinical departments and divisions. Through collaboration between top robotics experts and medical staff, the company develops robots that satisfy these unmet needs.
home.aboutHeadOffice: Head Office: 6F, Institute of Convergence Medical Technology, Seoul National University Hospital, 214 Yulgok-ro, Jongno-gu, Seoul, Korea
home.aboutRdCenter: R&D Center: Room 201, Gwanak S-Valley Startup Center, 597 Hoam-ro, Gwanak-gu, Seoul, Korea
home.rdItem1: Automated Assistance System for Medication Compounding
home.rdItem2: Robotic C-arm platform with holonomic motion and automatic repositioning
home.rdItem3: Upper Limb Power-Assist Wearable Robot for Daily Logistics Tasks
home.rdItem4: Power-assisted wheelchair with gravity and friction compensation
home.rdItem6: 4-axis moving platform for simulating respiratory motion in radiotherapy
home.rdItem7: Automatic immobilization board for breast cancer radiotherapy
home.rdItem8: Automatic abdominal breathing machines for quadriplegics or Pompe patients
home.rdItem9: Exoskeletal master device for dual arm robot teaching and teleoperation system
home.rdItem12: Kendo training robot and its motion teaching method
home.rdItem13: High power and precision smart actuators

company.heroDesc: Explore ROFORMAN lab research scenes, prototype demonstrations, and core technology capabilities.
company.researchCap1: Wearable assistive robot evaluation in a real lab environment.
company.researchCap2: Humanoid robot demonstration from earlier platform research.
company.coreTechDesc: We implement reliable robotics through intelligent control, stability validation, and medical-grade design.
company.techCap1: Foldable mobility platform prototype and compact transformation study.
company.techCap2: Wearable exoskeleton control test focused on precision and response stability.
company.compoundingTitle: Automated Assistance System for Medication Compounding
company.compoundingItem1: Reduces pharmacist workload and improves safety through advanced automation
company.compoundingItem2: Achieves high compounding precision with a peak-to-peak error of 0.09 %
company.compoundingItem3: Implements a plunger speed profile to eliminate cavitation-induced air bubbles in syringes
company.upperLimbTitle: Upper Limb Power-Assist Wearable Robot for Daily Logistics Tasks
company.upperLimbItem1: Provides precise vertical lifting assistance of 2 kgf per arm
company.upperLimbItem2: Features a torque control algorithm that compensates for link deflection and user posture
company.upperLimbItem3: Lightweight 6-DOF design enables a wide range of natural arm movements
company.wheelchairTitle: Power-assisted wheelchair with gravity and friction compensation
company.wheelchairItem1: Easy movement on slopes as on flat land
company.wheelchairItem3: Compensation of the friction force in the driving mechanism
company.stairItem1: Self-operating system with no human help
company.stairItem2: Measuring the shape of stairs on real-time
company.stairItem3: Small, simple, and stable leg mechanism
company.stairItem4: Comfortable ride due to linear climbing motion
company.fourAxisTitle: 4-axis moving platform for simulating respiratory motion in radiotherapy
company.fourAxisItem1: When performing the respiratory-gated radiation therapy (RGRT), the quality assurance (QA) process is required.
company.fourAxisItem2: Conventional moving platforms for QA generate 3D motions (xy motion and index motion), but for more precise QA, the motion platform should generate 4D motions (xyz motion and index motion).
company.immobilizationTitle: Automatic immobilization board for breast cancer radiotherapy
company.immobilizationItem1: Radiotherapy for breast cancer is conducted 28–33 times.
company.immobilizationItem2: To keep the pose of a patient at every treatment, the immobilization board is used.
company.immobilizationItem3: When using conventional immobilization boards, a therapist adjusts supporting parts manually. Because of time constraints, a therapist use only a few adjusting parts.
company.immobilizationItem4: The automatic immobilization board can control all the adjustments at the same time. By using it, a therapist can setup the pose of patients quickly and precisely.
company.abdominalTitle: Automatic abdominal breathing machines for quadriplegics or Pompe patients
company.abdominalItem1: This machine helps patients breathe by pushing and releasing the abdomen.
company.carmTitle: Robotic C-arm platform with holonomic motion and automatic repositioning
company.carmProblem1: Non-holonomic motion ⇒ Difficulty in position setting
company.carmSolutionTitle: We are developing a robotic C-arm platform, which can perform
company.carmSolution1: Precise holonomic motions with active casters, and
company.carmSolution2: Automatic repositioning with machine vision and laser sensing.
company.exoTitle: Exoskeletal master device for dual arm robot teaching and teleoperation system
company.exoSystemTitle: Robot teleoperation system, which consists of
company.exoSystem1: dual-arm slave robot and stair-climbing mobile platform,
company.humanoidItem2: Adjustable torsional stiffness of each joint
company.humanoidItem5: All-in-one solution consists of a motor driver, controller, and power module
company.oculomotorItem2: Peak velocity and acceleration: 800 deg/s, 30,000 deg/s²
company.oculomotorItem4: Can move in 3-DOF and rotates fast
company.oculomotorItem5: Same rotational radius as the human eyeball
company.oculomotorItem6: Can be installed in a human-head-sized curvy frame
company.kendoTitle: Kendo training robot and its motion teaching method
company.kendoItem1: Repeated exercise is essential to learn Kendo skills
company.kendoItem2: For kendo exercise, a partner is needed
company.kendoItem3: Robot can be an excellent assistant in Kendo training
company.kendoItem4: No need for worrying about injury by robot
company.kendoItem5: The ripple effect on technology is great
company.kendoItem6: Teaching of various complex Kendo motions
company.actuatorTitle: High power and precision smart actuators
company.actuatorItem1: High positioning accuracy enabled by the dual absolute encoder system
company.actuatorItem2: No need for a backup battery or a brake in securing output position
company.actuatorItem4: High mechanical accuracy and durability suitable for industrial robots
company.actuatorItem5: Hollow structure for easy cable wiring
company.actuatorItem6: Easy connection with other smart motors or robot frames
company.actuatorItem7: Short, cylindrical, symmetrical shape allowing compact assembly

product.heroDesc: Explore ROFORMAN products designed for real medical environments.
product.overviewDesc: Our solutions combine precision control and stability for use in clinical and research workflows.
product.cap1: Infusion pump prototype configured for controlled dose workflow testing.
product.cap2: Stair-climbing mobility platform tested in a step traversal scenario.

contact.heroDesc: Find quick answers to common questions below. If you need more help, use the contact information at the end of the page.
contact.a1: ROFORMAN develops robotics-related technologies and products for industrial and applied environments, including platform and integration support.
contact.q2: How can I request a product demo or partnership discussion?
contact.a2: Please send a short overview of your company, use case, and timeline to our contact email. We will route your inquiry to the relevant team.
contact.q3: Do you support technical collaboration with universities or research labs?
contact.a3: Yes. We are open to technical collaboration and joint research discussions depending on the project scope and alignment.
contact.q4: Where should I send technical support or integration questions?
contact.a4: Include your product/model information, issue summary, and any logs or images available in your email so we can respond more efficiently.

careers.heroDesc: Join ROFORMAN to build reliable robotics systems for medical and assistive environments.
careers.hiringStatusDesc: We are currently reviewing applications for engineering and product roles. Please check the openings below and apply by email with your resume and portfolio (if applicable).
careers.noJobs: There are no open positions at the moment. Please check back later.
careers.step1Body: Send your resume and a short introduction to info@roforman.com (portfolio / GitHub link recommended for engineering roles).
careers.step2Body: We review background, role fit, and project experience. Some roles may include a short technical discussion.
careers.step3Body: Role-specific interview with team members covering problem-solving, implementation experience, and collaboration style.
careers.step4Body: Successful candidates receive an offer and onboarding plan with role expectations and initial project scope.
